tone: Calm, technical, certification-led. The voice of an electrician who hands you the paperwork. Confident without being salesy; precise rather than chatty.
person: First person plural for the team voice (we / Pier and Jack); third person elsewhere (JP Electrical, they).
reading_level: Plain British English a homeowner or landlord reads easily; technical terms only where real (EICR, consumer unit, certificate, circuit).
do: Lead on correctness and certification: tested, signed off, certs handed over
Name Pier and Jack where it is true and useful (contact_name usage allowed, high confidence)
Use real electrical nouns: EICR, consumer unit, EV charger, circuit, fault
Stay specific: 'EICR booked and done within a week', 'all the relevant certs provided'
dont: No em/en dashes; use ' - ', comma, or full stop
No certification-scheme badges (NICEIC, NAPIT, Part P, 'fully insured') - none are evidenced
No generic slop: 'your trusted partner', 'professional service', 'competitive prices', 'quality you can rely on', 'your local experts', 'satisfaction guaranteed'
No meta-provenance ('reviews describe', 'sourced from Google', 'drawn from the verified listing')
No exclamation marks in authored copy (review quotes are verbatim and exempt)
No invented stats or photo counts; no fabricated certifications
headline_rule: Trade + location + the compliance/certified angle. Never a review quote, owner backstory, or platitude.
evidence_rule: Every visible claim traces to brief.json / source-evidence via a [src:] tag in copy-blocks.md.
